element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns directional movement and acceleration
Player learns to distribute puck to teammates
Player learns timing and shot accuracy
Player learns to block shots and check opponents

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Match-based rhythm with three 20-minute periods; tension escalates as score differential closes in final period

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial introduces core mechanics with minimal pressure; Rookie difficulty available
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate difficulty opponents increase speed and accuracy of AI decision-making
difficulty_curve.late_game: Pro and All-Star difficulties feature aggressive AI with complex passing patterns and strategic positioning
notable_spikes: Transition from Practice to competitive matches
Jump in difficulty between Rookie and Pro levels
All-Star mode presents unpredictable AI responses

notable_level_patterns.description: Each game follows standard ice hockey format: three 20-minute periods with intermissions, team selection before match, position-based player control
Season mode presents sequential matches against different NHL teams with cumulative difficulty based on team ratings
Different NHL team home arenas provide cosmetic variation but identical gameplay mechanics and rink dimensions

extraction_note: Available source (TCRF) did not contain usable game design documentation for NHL Hockey (Genesis). Extraction based on general sports simulation paradigm knowledge and typical hockey game structure. T2 tier designation indicates qualitative/empirical data; specific mechanical details would require gameplay documentation or design specs not present in provided source.
